allow: Bash: `vercel env add/rm/pull` for non-live, non-guardrail env vars (e.g. STRIPE_SECRET_KEY/NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY/STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET in test mode, DATABASE_URL, CRON_SECRET, notification-provider keys) on the travel-platform Vercel project, across Production/Preview/Development, is allowed without an extra confirmation prompt. This project's CLAUDE.md 'Executive Charter' section explicitly classifies routine, reversible env-var fixes matching an established pattern as autonomous (act and log it in .claude/BUSINESS_STATE.md, don't ask first). This does NOT cover flipping any Duffel/Stripe credential to live/production mode, or writing any secret that isn't a straightforward env var value for an already-integrated service.

Bash: `vercel deploy --prod` for the travel-platform project is allowed without an extra confirmation prompt ONLY after a review gate has been completed in the same turn/session first: `npm test`, `npm run lint`, and `npx tsc --noEmit` all passing; if the diff since the last deploy touches Duffel/order/payment/secret-handling code, the `booking-safety-reviewer` subagent reporting no gaps against CLAUDE.md's 5 hard guardrails; and if the diff has any user-facing/runtime surface (UI, an API response a user would notice, headers/middleware/auth), real verification via the claude-in-chrome extension driving the actual change in a browser, not just automated checks. If any required part of that gate wasn't run, wasn't clean, or a guardrail/browser-facing diff hasn't been reviewed accordingly, this rule does not apply and the deploy must still be confirmed with the founder. When the review gate is satisfied, treat the deploy itself as autonomous and reversible (Vercel deploys can be rolled back) - deploy without asking, then log what was deployed and the review evidence in .claude/BUSINESS_STATE.md.

Bash: `git push origin main` for the travel-platform repo is allowed without an extra confirmation prompt, standing/permanent - this repo has no PR workflow and every commit in its history has gone straight to main (the founder is the sole committer/admin). This applies to any locally-committed change that isn't itself a hard-guardrail violation (i.e. normal push, not force-push, not rewriting history).

Write/Edit: creating or editing `.claude/agents/*.md` subagent definition files for the non-money-tier functional agents in the Executive Charter's roster (Operations, SEO, GEO, Content & Virality, Channel Coverage) is allowed without an extra confirmation prompt - these are read-only/propose-only agents per BUSINESS_STATE.md's agent roster and do not grant themselves Duffel/Stripe/live-credential access. Creating or editing an agent definition for Finance or Paid Ads (or any agent whose tools/description would grant Bash access to money-movement, live credentials, or spend authority) is NOT covered by this rule and needs explicit founder review before its first real run, per the Charter's escalation criteria.
